untranslatable_reason: Pattern detection requires identifying twin peaks formed ~1 month apart, measuring the lowest low between them, and detecting whether a third peak forms before confirmation. These are multi-bar pattern recognition tasks that cannot be expressed as simple 3-field conditions on single indicators. The signal requires: (1) peak detection algorithm, (2) temporal spacing validation (month-apart), (3) lowest-low tracking between peaks, and (4) third-peak detection logic—none of which can be represented with allowed indicators and operators.
translation_notes: This is a classic chart pattern recognition signal from Bulkowski's work. While the directional bias (SHORT on breakdown below confirmation level) and success rates (72% bear, 19% avg decline) are well-defined, the entry mechanism is fundamentally a pattern shape detector rather than an indicator-based rule. A proper backtest would require custom pattern recognition code to identify Adam and Eve Double Tops before applying this rule.
